---
title: "Using Hexagonal Architecture for Mobile Applications"
author: "Prof. Dr. Robin Nunkesser"
format: revealjs
---

# Architectural Goals

## Overview

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

- Testability
- Separation of application and domain logic
- Loosely coupled User Interface
- Independence
    + of external frameworks
    + of external agencies

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

![](../../images/unsplash/joshua-earle-Dwheufds6kQ-unsplash.jpg)

<span style='font-size: xx-small;'>Photo by <a href="https://unsplash.com/@joshuaearle?utm_source=unsplash&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=creditCopyText">Joshua Earle</a> on <a href="https://unsplash.com/s/photos/overview?utm_source=unsplash&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=creditCopyText">Unsplash</a></span>

:::
::::

    
## Mobile Applications

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

- No different goals from general applications
- Difference in testability
- Existence of code not runnable on development hardware 

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

![](../../images/unsplash/balazs-ketyi-b9rPuUQ_YSs-unsplash.jpg)

<span style='font-size: xx-small;'>Photo by <a href="https://unsplash.com/@balazsketyi?utm_source=unsplash&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=creditCopyText">Balázs Kétyi</a> on <a href="https://unsplash.com/s/photos/mobile-application?utm_source=unsplash&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=creditCopyText">Unsplash</a></span>

:::
::::

# Architectural Choices

## Overview

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

- MVX (MVC, MVVM, MVP, MVU, ...)
- Mobile-only Architecture
- General Software Architecture

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

![](../../images/unsplash/joshua-earle-Dwheufds6kQ-unsplash.jpg)

<span style='font-size: xx-small;'>Photo by <a href="https://unsplash.com/@joshuaearle?utm_source=unsplash&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=creditCopyText">Joshua Earle</a> on <a href="https://unsplash.com/s/photos/overview?utm_source=unsplash&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=creditCopyText">Unsplash</a></span>

:::
::::


## MVX

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

- Not an Architecture but a high-level design pattern
- Not sufficient for non trivial apps
- May be combined with a software architecture

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

![](../../images/sd/black/applemvc.svg)

:::
::::

## Mobile-only Architecture

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

- Often OS specific
- Often tightly integrated with only one shape of MVX

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

![](../../images/sd/black/mobileonlyarch.svg)

:::
::::


## Software Architecture

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

- Not clear if fitting for mobile applications
- Scant research

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

![](../../images/sd/black/bci.svg)

:::
::::

# Choosing a Software Architecture

## Overview

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

- BCE (Boundary-Control-Entity) 
- DDD (Domain Driven Design)
- Hexagonal Architecture (aka Ports and Adapters)
- Onion Architecture
- Clean Architecture

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

![](../../images/unsplash/joshua-earle-Dwheufds6kQ-unsplash.jpg)

<span style='font-size: xx-small;'>Photo by <a href="https://unsplash.com/@joshuaearle?utm_source=unsplash&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=creditCopyText">Joshua Earle</a> on <a href="https://unsplash.com/s/photos/overview?utm_source=unsplash&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=creditCopyText">Unsplash</a></span>

:::
::::


## Clean Architecture

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

- Very popular
- Many misleading mobile implementations

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

![](../../images/sd/cleanarchcolor.png)

:::
::::

## Hexagonal Architecture

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

- Shares base with clean architecture
- Easy to implement and extend

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

![](../../images/sd/hexarchcolor.png)

:::
::::


# Considerations for Mobile

## Overview

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

- Modularity
- Dependencies
- Concurrency
- Platform Dependency

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

![](../../images/unsplash/joshua-earle-Dwheufds6kQ-unsplash.jpg)

<span style='font-size: xx-small;'>Photo by <a href="https://unsplash.com/@joshuaearle?utm_source=unsplash&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=creditCopyText">Joshua Earle</a> on <a href="https://unsplash.com/s/photos/overview?utm_source=unsplash&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=creditCopyText">Unsplash</a></span>

:::
::::


## Modularity

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

- Seperate into packages
- Use built-in approach

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

![](../../images/unsplash/ashkan-forouzani-m0l9NBCivuk-unsplash.jpg)

<span style='font-size: xx-small;'>Photo by <a href="https://unsplash.com/@ashkfor121?utm_source=unsplash&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=creditCopyText">Ashkan Forouzani</a> on <a href="https://unsplash.com/s/photos/puzzle?utm_source=unsplash&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=creditCopyText">Unsplash</a></span>

:::
::::
  

## Dependencies

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

Either

- Dependency Injection Framework
- Manually configurable dependencies

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

![](../../images/unsplash/sara-bakhshi-MfnX4XtGnvU-unsplash.jpg)

<span style='font-size: xx-small;'>Photo by <a href="https://unsplash.com/@sarabakhshi?utm_source=unsplash&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=creditCopyText">Sara Bakhshi</a> on <a href="https://unsplash.com/s/photos/injection?utm_source=unsplash&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=creditCopyText">Unsplash</a></span>

:::
::::
  

## Concurrency

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

- Use newest built-in general approach
- Should be part of the ports

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

![](../../images/unsplash/faris-mohammed-Rqv4AWbvPjg-unsplash.jpg)

<span style='font-size: xx-small;'>Photo by <a href="https://unsplash.com/@pkmfaris?utm_source=unsplash&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=creditCopyText">Faris Mohammed</a> on <a href="https://unsplash.com/s/photos/parallel?utm_source=unsplash&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=creditCopyText">Unsplash</a></span>

:::
::::
  

## Platform Dependency

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

- Application is always independent
- UI may be dependent
- Infrastructure should be split into dependent and independent parts

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

![](../../images/unsplash/lee-easton-wfvUPYFeyvo-unsplash.jpg)

<span style='font-size: xx-small;'>Photo by <a href="https://unsplash.com/@leeston?utm_source=unsplash&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=creditCopyText">Lee Easton</a> on <a href="https://unsplash.com/s/photos/platform?utm_source=unsplash&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=creditCopyText">Unsplash</a></span>

:::
::::

# Conclusion

##

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

- Most mobile applications should use a software architecture
- Hexagonal Architecture is recommended
    + Achieves architectural goals
    + Flexible
    + Extendible
- Mobile specific challenges can be addressed

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

![](../../images/unsplash/patrick-perkins-Pg44v2M2S6k-unsplash.jpg)

<span style='font-size: xx-small;'>Photo by <a href="https://unsplash.com/@patrickperkins?utm_source=unsplash&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=creditCopyText">Patrick Perkins</a> on <a href="https://unsplash.com/s/photos/end?utm_source=unsplash&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=creditCopyText">Unsplash</a></span>

:::
::::
